title: tritomium uvaria
side_effects: Tritomium uvaria can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and stomach irritation in individuals who consume it. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also lead to kidney damage and impaired liver function. Some users may experience allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling, especially when applied topically. In severe cases, improper use of tritomium uvaria can lead to seizures and respiratory failure.
